message:
test(lifecycle): index checkpoint + fingerprint shape (ibuuh.24)

ibuuh.24 crash-safety row. The world-class stale-refresh architecture
promises crash-safe resume: a rebuild that crashed mid-way can be
resumed because state.index.checkpoint + state.index.fingerprint carry
enough info to decide 'resume vs start over'. If any of those fields
rename or drop, the resume logic silently loses the signal it needs
and either re-starts from scratch (wasted work) or resumes against a
mismatched DB (correctness risk).

New test index_checkpoint_and_fingerprint_blocks_have_stable_shape
pins:
 * checkpoint is an object
 * checkpoint.present is always a bool
 * checkpoint.{completed, db_matches, schema_matches,
   page_size_matches, page_size_compatible} are each bool-or-null
 * when checkpoint.present=false, ALL bool-or-null metadata fields
   MUST be null (absent checkpoint => absent checkpoint metadata —
   catches a stale-cache class where metadata lingers past reset)
 * fingerprint is an object
 * fingerprint.current_db_fingerprint / checkpoint_fingerprint are
   each string-or-null
 * fingerprint.matches_current_db_fingerprint is bool-or-null

VERIFICATION:
cargo test --test lifecycle_matrix index_checkpoint_and_fingerprint:
1/1 pass.

Follow-up slice on coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.24; bead stays
open for the rest of the refresh architecture.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
